assertions.expectation: Categorizes notifications by type: auto-upgrade scheduled, new version available, end-of-life/deprecation, security patch
assertions.reason: Response clearly categorizes all notification types with dedicated sections for auto-upgrade scheduled, new version available, end-of-life warnings, security patches, and additionally covers deprecated API usage

assertions.expectation: Assigns urgency levels to each category (security patches = highest, new version available = lowest)
assertions.reason: Response uses clear urgency indicators: deprecated API = 🚨 URGENT, EoS warnings = 🚨 PLAN, security patches = ⚠️ EVALUATE with CVSS-based priority, new version = ⚠️ EVALUATE (lowest action priority), auto-upgrade = ✅ MONITOR

assertions.expectation: End-of-life warnings require action: plan upgrade before the support window closes
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Action required: Plan upgrade before EoS date' and provides detailed timeline guidance with specific actions for different time windows (90+ days, 30-60 days, <30 days)

assertions.expectation: Auto-upgrade scheduled notifications should trigger pre-upgrade checklist execution
assertions.reason: While the response covers when to act on auto-upgrade notifications (code freeze, timing adjustments), it does not mention executing a pre-upgrade checklist as a standard procedure

assertions.expectation: Security patch notifications may require expedited upgrades outside normal maintenance windows
assertions.reason: Response clearly states critical CVEs require upgrade within 7 days, high CVEs within 30 days, and provides accelerated patching commands for faster security patch rollout

assertions.expectation: New version available notifications are informational — use for planning, not immediate action
assertions.reason: Response marks new version notifications as ⚠️ EVALUATE with 'Default action: Wait for auto-upgrade (recommended)' and positions them for planning rather than immediate action

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting up Pub/Sub or Cloud Monitoring alerts for GKE upgrade events
assertions.reason: Response includes a dedicated 'Cloud Monitoring Alert Policies' section recommending creating separate alert policies for different notification types, though it doesn't specifically mention Pub/Sub

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE release notes as the source for understanding what changed in each version
assertions.reason: Response references checking 'release notes' when evaluating new version notifications to understand critical features/fixes
